pr.title: Match/improve 52 functions across 16 TUs (1 at 100%, 35 at >=90%)
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Broad decomp-matching batch for many previously placeholder functions across fighter/Kirby, CPU attack AI, game camera/unlock code, stage/ground code, trophy-list UI, lbarq, hsd_3AA7 card-state code, and synth SFX readdressing. The PR body reports 52 functions across 16 TUs with 1 exact match and 35 at >=90%, and the bot report showed matched-code/data gains. A late review fix reverted toy.c back to upstream after a layout-only data-section shift broke un_80309338; the final slice still contains toy.h changes but no toy.c diff, so the advertised toy.c/function-count details appear partly stale.

key_files.why: Added 7 Kirby SpecialN/copy-ability bodies, including asset/archive loading, hat DObj/JObj setup, cleanup, and copy-ability article registration.
Added 4 Kirby/Yoshi SpecialN bodies; used stack padding and dont_inline pragmas for matching.
Added ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220 for CPU attack/defense item hitbox decisions; required richer Fighter_804D64FC and Fighter_x1A88 typing.
Added several Vs camera/menu camera bodies and static float table data, plus archive/SIS/JObj setup includes.
Added Ground_801C20E0 light override filtering, Ground_801C28CC BGM parameter expansion, and Ground_801C34AC joint mapping logic.
Largest stage addition in the final diff; added Venom stage/Arwing-related bodies and needed player, grmaterial, and itarwinglaser includes.
Added Fourside UFO/helicopter/render callback logic, including the PR's only 100% body match fn_801F3F74 per PR body.
Added Kongo stage bodies and removed placeholder include; new bodies were cited as source of harmless sdata2 displacement regressions.
Added material TEV/color-overlay setup and recursive MObj class replacement logic.
Added 6 trophy-list UI bodies plus local TyList row/GObj helper structs and additional HSD/lb includes.
Added 6 memory-card/card-state functions; comments document remaining mwcc codegen blockers.
Added lbArq_80014BD0 ARQ request/free-list handling and typed callback signature.
Final diff only adds externs; issue comment says toy.c itself was reverted to upstream to fix a layout regression.

what_changed: Replaced many `/// #funcname` placeholders with C bodies across the final 16 C translation units, concentrated in Kirby copy-ability specials, CPU attack choice logic, gm camera/unlock code, stage code, trophy-list code, lbarq, hsd_3AA7, and synth.

Updated headers and prototypes away from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, including `fn_800DA8E4(Fighter_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, s32)`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438` returning void, typed ftcpuattack helper prototypes, typed hsd_3AA7 prototypes, and `lbArq_80014BD0` using `lbArqCallback`.

Recovered or refined several structs/field layouts: `Fighter_804D64FC_t` table fields, `Fighter_x1A88_t` attack/defense queues, `UnkStageDat` light override fields, `TyListArg`, `TyListState`, local `TyListRow`, `TyListGobjEntry`, `LightOverrideEntry`, `LightOverrideFlags`, and `CardQueueEntry`.

Added strict-prototype/build includes surfaced by CI, such as `ft/ftmaterial.h`, `lb/lbarchive.h`, `lb/lbdvd.h`, `<baselib/id.h>`, `ft/ftlib.h`, `gr/grmaterial.h`, `it/items/itarwinglaser.h`, `<baselib/debug.h>`, `lb/lbaudio_ax.h`, and `mn/mnmain.h`.
Removed or changed several artificial `PAD_STACK`/assert patterns in stage files like gricemt, grkongo, and grpura where the final diff shows cleanup around existing matched/near-matched bodies.
An earlier ftCo_0A01.c part of the batch was reverted because it broke three 100% matches and severely degraded four near-matches; the PR body says those functions need a separate approach.

A review fix reverted toy.c to upstream after extra struct redefs, include reorder, and externs shifted toy.o data by about 1.3KB and regressed `un_80309338`; the final changed-files list contains `toy.h` but no `toy.c`.
smart_moves: The author documented a match-tier breakdown and explicitly framed sub-80% functions as structurally useful permuter starting points rather than pretending they were good matches.
When toy.c layout churn broke an existing 100% match, the fix was to revert toy.c wholesale to upstream instead of chasing a semantically irrelevant sdata2 displacement diff.

The review response correctly identified that many tiny regressions were caused by new literals/string data in the same TU changing sdata2/literal-pool offsets, while regressing function bodies remained unchanged.
The batch paired new bodies with required prototype/header/type work so strict CI flags (`--max-errors 0 --require-protos --map`) could pass.

Several difficult bodies deliberately preserve mwcc-sensitive source shape: `#pragma dont_inline`, `PAD_STACK`/`FORCE_PAD_STACK`, byte-offset casts, local temporary structs, goto labels, and manual pointer walks.
hsd_3AA7 comments record specific remaining codegen blockers such as `rlwimi`/`srawi`/`clrlwi`, dead branch emission, regalloc swaps, and absorption order, making future permuter work more targeted.
The author reported adding a local `tools/verify_pr.py` workflow to compare against upstream/master before pushing future PRs, aimed at catching layout-only regressions earlier.
decomp_lessons: Adding source for a former placeholder can change a TU's local string/float literals and sdata2 layout; neighboring functions may lose match bytes only because 16-bit displacement operands changed.

Do not let unrelated include reorder, local struct redefs, or new extern blocks drift a TU from upstream layout; in this PR, toy.c drift moved data enough to break `un_80309338` even though its body was identical.

For large batch PRs, split or revert risky TUs when they break existing 100% matches; the ftCo_0A01.c rollback shows that preserving upstream matches can be more valuable than landing many partial bodies at once.
Prototype recovery is part of matching: strict `--require-protos` builds force unknown-call cleanup and often expose real return/argument types needed by later decomp work.
Use offset-preserving struct refinement incrementally: add named fields where demanded by bodies, but keep `xNN` field names and pads when semantics are still uncertain.
Low-percentage but structurally faithful C can be useful if it records control flow, data layout, and codegen blockers for permuter work; label the weakness clearly.
Final PR bodies can become stale after late revert commits; future postmortems should cross-check title/body function counts against the final changed-files/diff.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named functions keep address-based names such as `ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34`, `gmCamera_801A2D44`, `grVenom_8020362C`, `un_80312904`, `fn_80313BD8`, and `hsd_803B2374`.
Stage functions retain module prefixes: `Ground_`, `grFourside_`, `grKongo_`, `grMaterial_`, `grPura_`, `grVenom_`, and `grIceMt_`.
`fn_` is used for anonymous helpers and `un_` for trophy/unclassified symbols, while HSD/baselib symbols keep `HSD_` or `hsd_` prefixes.
Recovered types use module-local names when semantics are limited, e.g. `TyListRow`, `TyListGobjEntry`, `LightOverrideEntry`, `LightOverrideFlags`, and `CardQueueEntry`.
Offset-style fields remain when semantics are partial (`xA8_array`, `xCC_array`, `x270`, `unk18`, `unk1C`), often with comments describing observed use instead of over-renaming.
Header prototypes continue the project convention of address comments like `/* 0EF438 */` beside each declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma dont_inline reset` around functions such as `ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0` and `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801093A0` to control mwcc inlining.
Used `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK` where stack frame shape mattered, while also removing old artificial padding in some stage functions when it no longer matched.
Used byte-pointer arithmetic and explicit casts for uncertain layouts, including Ground light override arrays, synth SFX group entries, Kirby hat DObj arrays, and trophy-list row data.
Used local packed helper structs instead of fully globalizing uncertain layouts, preserving offsets while making code readable enough for future refinement.
Preserved mwcc-like control flow with gotos, early returns, manual loops, and hand-unrolled zeroing in places where natural C would likely schedule/register-allocate differently.
Tracked data-section effects as a matching issue: the author explained sdata2/literal-pool reorder regressions and fixed the worst case by restoring toy.c's upstream layout.
Documented specific unmatched assembly causes in comments for hsd_3AA7, giving future permuter searches concrete targets.
review_feedback: No formal GitHub review records or inline review comments are present in the slice.

decomp-dev bot reported matched code rising to 66.51% (+0.04%, +1404 bytes) and matched data to 38.81% (+0.01%, +112 bytes), with 7 new matches, 55 unmatched-item improvements, and 21 unmatched-item regressions in its report.
The author thanked reviewers and pushed commit `85d5a32` to fix `un_80309338` regressing from 100% to 97.65% by reverting toy.c to upstream.
The author explained remaining tiny regressions as expected sdata2/literal-pool reorder artifacts from adding bodies into host TUs; all were described as under 0.15% and semantically unchanged.
The author offered to revert additional TUs if reviewers preferred a cleaner regression report over keeping the 17 added matches in those files.
follow_up_queries: Verify the final function count against the merged diff: the PR body/title mention 52 functions and toy.c additions, but the final slice shows toy.c reverted and absent from changed files.
Revisit the reverted `ftCo_0A01.c` functions in a separate PR using layout-safe variants, permuter work, or NON_MATCHING gating.

Permute low-tier bodies called out in the PR body, especially `ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50`, `gmCamera_801A292C`, `Ground_801C28CC`, `grVenom_8020362C`, `ftCo_800BB220`, `hsd_803AC558`, and `hsd_803B2550`.
Audit remaining tiny sdata2/literal-pool regressions in grkongo, gricemt, grvenom, grpura, grfourside, and ftcpuattack by comparing unchanged function bodies against changed load-offset operands.
Confirm the PR-body note about `gm_8016A9E8` in `gm_1601.h`; that header is not present in the final changed-files list from this slice.
Consider moving the grVenom `grVe_Data`/ground-data TODO structs into a proper shared stage header only when more users require them.
